response.justification: Bogdan has genuine IDE and developer tooling experience (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI) and strong architectural skills for complex systems, but there is a significant expertise gap in the core technical requirements. His most recent and substantial Java work was over a decade ago (2014-2015 at NI, some 2016-2018 at Altom), while his current focus (re:cinq, Optilyz) is TypeScript/Node.js. There is no demonstrated experience with Spring, Kotlin, or modern JVM enterprise frameworks—the role's key focus areas. His adaptability and systems thinking could translate, but he would require substantial onboarding on the JVM ecosystem.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI, a production IDE used by engineers worldwide
Proven ability to design and scale complex systems (microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec, distributed infrastructure at multiple roles)
Strong software craftsmanship focus with TDD, clean architecture, and open-source contributions (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts testing libraries) demonstrating deep systems thinking
concerns: No demonstrated experience with Java, Kotlin, Spring, or other enterprise JVM frameworks—the core technical focus of the role. Java is listed in skills but not substantiated in recent work history.

Most recent Java work was 10+ years ago (National Instruments 2014-2015, some Altom robotic work 2016-2018). Current focus (April 2025-present at re:cinq, 2021-2025 at Optilyz) is heavily TypeScript/Node.js, representing a significant technical drift.
No evidence of familiarity with JVM internals, language tooling infrastructure (e.g., language servers, compilation pipelines), or IDE platform architecture beyond high-level IDE product experience.
